name: Configuration Hardening
def: Changing default, permissive configurations of systems, services, and platforms to a secure, least-functionality baseline.
problem: Out-of-the-box defaults favour ease of use over security: open ports, default credentials, verbose errors, broad permissions.
mechanism: Apply hardening baselines (CIS Benchmarks, vendor guides): disable unused services, change/disable default accounts, restrict permissions and network exposure, enforce TLS, turn off debug/verbose output, and continuously audit against the baseline (often automated as code).
failures: Hardening once, then config drifting back
whenNot: Never skip for production/internet-facing systems; tune depth to risk
myths: "Defaults are secure" — vendor defaults optimise for setup, not security
